text_block_list.name: Reporter Otonashi
text_block_list.text:
Now then, this month's special will cover the
Senior Spring Double Crown—the spring Tenno
Sho and Takarazuka Kinen.

text_block_list.name: Reporter Otonashi
text_block_list.text:
With one being 3,200 meters and the other
2,200, they obviously call for different
strategies, which I hope to learn more about
through interviews...

text_block_list.name: Kitasan Black
text_block_list.text:
What's different between the spring Tenno Sho
and the Takarazuka Kinen? What do you think,
Inari One?

text_block_list.name: Inari One
text_block_list.text:
Don't be a goof! There is no difference! At the
end of the day, both races are...

text_block_list.name: Inari & Kitasan
text_block_list.text: A spring festival!

text_block_list.name: Kitasan Black
text_block_list.text: I mean, what else could they be?

text_block_list.name: Inari One
text_block_list.text:
Right?! All that matters is winning and making the
crowd go wild!

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
The spring Tenno Sho and Takarazuka Kinen...
Well, from my perspective, they are the same.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
Regardless of the distance, my winning equation
will bring me to victory.

text_block_list.name: T.M. Opera O
text_block_list.text: I couldn't agree more.

text_block_list.name: T.M. Opera O
text_block_list.text: No matter the distance, it is I who takes the turf! I who emerges triumphant! I who suffers defeat! I am the common denominator!

text_block_list.name: T.M. Opera O
text_block_list.text:
The season of spring speaks to my character. Of
course that goes for summer, fall, and winter too!
Haaah ha ha!

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
...I'm quite surprised to see that our opinions
align.

text_block_list.name: Reporter Otonashi
text_block_list.text:
(I couldn't find a runner who saw a difference. Is
winning both races only possible by not worrying
about things as major as distance?)

text_block_list.name: Reporter Otonashi
text_block_list.text: (There must be someone who sees them differently...)

text_block_list.name: Tamamo Cross
text_block_list.text:
They're different if ya ask me. Completely
different. The difference is clear as day.

text_block_list.name: Reporter Otonashi
text_block_list.text:
(Finally! I knew there'd be someone who sees the
difference!)

text_block_list.name: Tamamo Cross
text_block_list.text:
The Takarazuka Kinen's at Hanshin, which is
basically Osaka! And the spring Tenno Sho's in
Kyoto! They're nothing alike!

text_block_list.name: Tamamo Cross
text_block_list.text:
First of all, the food's way too expensive in
Kyoto. I can't afford to eat anything over there!

text_block_list.name: Tamamo Cross
text_block_list.text:
Basically, the Takarazuka Kinen's home, and the
spring Tenno Sho's away! Got it?

text_block_list.name: Reporter Otonashi
text_block_list.text:
I've made a wonderful discovery! The two races
differ not just in length, but also in their image
and culture as well!

text_block_list.name: Reporter Otonashi
text_block_list.text: I must dig deeper into this perspective...

text_block_list.name: Reporter Otonashi
text_block_list.text: Time for a food trip around Kyoto and Osaka!
